html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Thirteen — Part One

The Book of Shemu'el Bet

SHEMU'EL BET

The Second Book of Samuel

Chapters 1 through 6

Dawid's Lament — King Over All Yisra'EL — The Ark Comes to Yerushalayim

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QSama (4Q51) — Cave 4 — older text tradition than Masoretic

4QSamb (4Q52), 4QSamc (4Q53) — Cave 4

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 6 — DAWID'S RISE TO THE FULL KINGDOM

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 1 The Death of Sha'ul Reported — Dawid's Lament — How the Mighty Have Fallen

1 After the death of Sha'ul, when Dawid had returned from striking down the Amalekites, Dawid remained two days in Tziklag.

2 And on the third day, behold, a man came from Sha'ul's camp, with his clothes torn and dirt on his head. And when he came to Dawid, he fell to the ground and paid homage.

3 Dawid said to him, Where do you come from? And he said to him, I have escaped from the camp of Yisra'EL.

4 And Dawid said to him, How did it go? Tell me. And he answered, The people fled from the battle, and also many of the people have fallen and are dead, and Sha'ul and his son Yonatan are also dead.

5 Then Dawid said to the young man who told him, How do you know that Sha'ul and his son Yonatan are dead?

6 And the young man who told him said, By chance I happened to be on Mount Gilboa, and there was Sha'ul leaning on his spear, and behold, the chariots and the horsemen were close upon him.

7 And when he looked behind him, he saw me, and called to me. And I answered, Here I am.

8 And he said to me, Who are you? I answered him, I am an Amalekite.

9 And he said to me, Stand beside me and kill me, for anguish has seized me, and yet my life still lingers.

10 So I stood beside him and killed him, because I was sure that he could not live after he had fallen. And I took the crown that was on his head and the armlet that was on his arm, and I have brought them here to my lord.

11 Then Dawid took hold of his clothes and tore them, and so did all the men who were with him.

12 And they mourned and wept and fasted until evening for Sha'ul and for Yonatan his son and for the people of YHWH and for the house of Yisra'EL, because they had fallen by the sword.

13 And Dawid said to the young man who told him, Where do you come from? And he answered, I am the son of a sojourner, an Amalekite.

14 Dawid said to him, How is it you were not afraid to put out your hand to destroy YHWH's anointed?

15 Then Dawid called one of the young men and said, Go, execute him. And he struck him down so that he died.

16 And Dawid said to him, Your blood be on your head, for your own mouth has testified against you, saying, I have killed YHWH's anointed.

17 And Dawid lamented with this lamentation over Sha'ul and Yonatan his son,

18 And he said it should be taught to the people of Yehudah — behold, it is written in the Book of the Upright.

19 Your glory, O Yisra'EL, is slain on your high places. How the mighty have fallen.

20 Tell it not in Gat, publish it not in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Pelishtim rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised exult.

21 You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain upon you, nor fields of offerings, for there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Sha'ul, not anointed with oil.

22 From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Yonatan turned not back, and the sword of Sha'ul returned not empty.

23 Sha'ul and Yonatan, beloved and lovely, in life and in death they were not divided — they were swifter than eagles, they were stronger than lions.

24 You daughters of Yisra'EL, weep over Sha'ul, who clothed you luxuriously in scarlet, who put ornaments of gold on your apparel.

25 How the mighty have fallen in the midst of the battle. Yonatan lies slain on your high places.

26 I am distressed for you, my brother Yonatan — very pleasant have you been to me. Your love to me was extraordinary, surpassing the love of women.

27 How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war perished.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama (4Q51) preserves portions of Shemu'el Bet. Chapter 1 opens with the covenant lament of Dawid — the Qinah — over Sha'ul and Yonatan. The reference to the Book of the Upright — Sefer HaYashar — in verse 18 is the same ancient source cited in Yehoshua 10:13, confirming an early collection of covenant poetry.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Shemu'el Bet in full. Verse 26 — your love to me was extraordinary, surpassing the love of women — is read as the covenant declaration of the bond between Dawid and Yonatan: a covenant of the soul that exceeded every natural relational bond.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Shemu'el Bet with a text tradition frequently older and more complete than the Masoretic. Chapter 1 is largely consistent between the DSS and Masoretic traditions. The Amalekite's claim to have killed Sha'ul contradicts Chapter 31:4-5 of Shemu'el Aleph — he fabricated the story hoping for reward.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — how is it you were not afraid to put out your hand to destroy YHWH's anointed? Dawid's question to the Amalekite who claimed to have killed Sha'ul is the same covenant principle he applied twice in the wilderness. The anointing is YHWH's act and only YHWH's judgment can end it. Dawid executes the man not for killing an enemy but for claiming to have violated the covenant of the anointed. Verse 19-27 — the Qinah — the covenant lament — is one of the most beautiful elegies in the Hebrew canon. Dawid mourns Sha'ul without covenant bitterness and Yonatan with covenant grief that has no parallel in the Nevi'im.

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 2 Dawid Anointed King in Khevron — Ish-Boshet Made King — Avner and Yoav at the Pool of Givon

1 After this Dawid inquired of YHWH, Shall I go up into any of the cities of Yehudah? And YHWH said to him, Go up. Dawid said, To which shall I go up? And he said, To Khevron.

2 So Dawid went up there, and his two wives also, Akhinoam of Yizre'el and Avigayil the widow of Naval of Karmel.

3 And Dawid brought up his men who were with him, every one with his household, and they lived in the towns of Khevron.

4 And the men of Yehudah came, and there they anointed Dawid king over the house of Yehudah. When they told Dawid, The men of Yavesh-Gil'ad were those who buried Sha'ul,

5 Dawid sent messengers to the men of Yavesh-Gil'ad and said to them, May you be blessed by YHWH, because you showed this loyalty to Sha'ul your lord and buried him.

6 Now may YHWH show steadfast love and faithfulness to you. And I will do good to you because you have done this thing.

7 Now therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant, for Sha'ul your lord is dead, and the house of Yehudah has anointed me king over them.

8 But Avner the son of Ner, commander of Sha'ul's army, had taken Ish-Boshet the son of Sha'ul and brought him over to Makhanayim.

9 And he made him king over Gil'ad and the Ashurites and Yizre'el and Efrayim and Binyamin and all Yisra'EL.

10 Ish-Boshet, Sha'ul's son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Yisra'EL, and he reigned two years. But the house of Yehudah followed Dawid.

11 And the time that Dawid was king in Khevron over the house of Yehudah was seven years and six months.

12 Avner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ish-Boshet the son of Sha'ul, went out from Makhanayim to Giv'on.

13 And Yoav the son of Tzeruya and the servants of Dawid went out and met them at the pool of Giv'on. And they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool.

14 And Avner said to Yoav, Let the young men arise and compete before us. And Yoav said, Let them arise.

15 Then they arose and passed over by number, twelve for Binyamin and Ish-Boshet the son of Sha'ul, and twelve of the servants of Dawid.

16 And each caught his opponent by the head and thrust his sword in his opponent's side, so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Khelkat-Hatzurim, which is at Giv'on.

17 And the battle was very fierce that day. And Avner and the men of Yisra'EL were beaten before the servants of Dawid.

18 And the three sons of Tzeruya were there, Yoav, Avishai, and Asah'el. Now Asah'el was as swift of foot as a wild gazelle.

19 And Asah'el pursued Avner, and as he went, he turned neither to the right hand nor to the left from following Avner.

20 Then Avner looked behind him and said, Is it you, Asah'el? And he answered, It is I.

21 Avner said to him, Turn aside to your right hand or to your left, and seize one of the young men and take his spoil. But Asah'el would not turn aside from following him.

22 And Avner said again to Asah'el, Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I lift up my face to your brother Yoav?

23 But he refused to turn aside. Therefore Avner struck him in the stomach with the butt of his spear, so that the spear came out at his back. And he fell there and died where he was. And all who came to the place where Asah'el had fallen and died, stood still.

24 But Yoav and Avishai pursued Avner. And as the sun was going down they came to the hill of Ammah, which lies before Giyakh on the way to the wilderness of Giv'on.

25 And the people of Binyamin gathered themselves together behind Avner and became one group and took their stand on the top of a hill.

26 Then Avner called to Yoav, Shall the sword devour forever? Do you not know that the end will be bitter? How long will it be before you tell your people to turn from the pursuit of their brothers?

27 And Yoav said, As Creator lives, if you had not spoken, surely the men would not have given up the pursuit of their brothers until the morning.

28 So Yoav blew the trumpet, and all the men stopped and pursued Yisra'EL no more, nor did they fight anymore.

29 And Avner and his men went through the Aravah all that night, and they crossed the Yarden, and marching the whole morning they came to Makhanayim.

30 Yoav returned from the pursuit of Avner, and when he had gathered all the people together, there were missing from Dawid's servants nineteen men besides Asah'el.

31 But the servants of Dawid had struck down of Binyamin three hundred and sixty of Avner's men.

32 And they took up Asah'el and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was at Beit-Lekhem. And Yoav and his men marched all night, and the day broke upon them at Khevron.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 2. The covenant division of the kingdom — Dawid over Yehudah in Khevron, Ish-Boshet over the northern tribes from Makhanayim — establishes the political reality of the transition period. Dawid is anointed by covenant process. Ish-Boshet is installed by Avner's military power.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Dawid's first act as king of Yehudah — verse 5-6 — is to honor the men of Yavesh-Gil'ad for burying Sha'ul. The covenant king begins his reign by acknowledging covenant loyalty shown to his predecessor and enemy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 2 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The civil war between the house of Sha'ul and the house of Dawid — the longest sustained military conflict in the Dawid narrative — begins here. Seven and a half years of covenant division before the full kingdom is united.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26 — shall the sword devour forever? Avner's question to Yoav is the covenant voice of reason in the middle of fratricidal war. The men fighting are brothers — Yisra'ELites on both sides. The sword that devours the covenant community produces only bitterness. Yoav answers with the covenant logic of restraint but will not forget the personal debt — Asah'el his brother is dead. The covenant tension between tribal loyalty and national covenant unity runs through every chapter of the civil war period. Verse 5-6 — Dawid blesses the men of Yavesh-Gil'ad. The first diplomatic act of the new king is covenant honor to those who honored his predecessor. This is covenant magnanimity that distinguishes Dawid from the political logic of his time.

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 3 Avner Defects to Dawid — Yoav Kills Avner — Dawid's Lament — I Am This Day Weak

1 There was a long war between the house of Sha'ul and the house of Dawid. And Dawid grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Sha'ul became weaker and weaker.

2 And sons were born to Dawid at Khevron: his firstborn was Amnon, of Akhinoam of Yizre'el.

3 And his second, Khileyav, of Avigayil the widow of Naval of Karmel; and the third, Avshalom the son of Ma'akhah, the daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur;

4 And the fourth, Adoniyah the son of Khaggit; and the fifth, Shefatyah the son of Avital;

5 And the sixth, Yitream, of Eglah, Dawid's wife. These were born to Dawid in Khevron.

6 While there was war between the house of Sha'ul and the house of Dawid, Avner was making himself strong in the house of Sha'ul.

7 Now Sha'ul had a concubine whose name was Ritzpah, the daughter of Ayah. And Ish-Boshet said to Avner, Why have you gone in to my father's concubine?

8 Then Avner was very angry over the words of Ish-Boshet and said, Am I a dog's head of Yehudah? To this day I keep showing steadfast love to the house of Sha'ul your father, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not given you into the hand of Dawid. And yet you charge me today with a fault concerning a woman.

9 Creator do so to Avner and more also, if I do not accomplish for Dawid what YHWH has sworn to him,

10 To transfer the kingdom from the house of Sha'ul and set up the throne of Dawid over Yisra'EL and over Yehudah, from Dan to Be'er-Sheva.

11 And Ish-Boshet could not answer Avner another word, because he feared him.

12 And Avner sent messengers to Dawid on his behalf, saying, To whom does the land belong? Make your covenant with me, and behold, my hand shall be with you to bring over all Yisra'EL to you.

13 And he said, Good — I will make a covenant with you. But one thing I require of you — that is, you shall not see my face unless you first bring Mikhal, Sha'ul's daughter, when you come to see my face.

14 Then Dawid sent messengers to Ish-Boshet, Sha'ul's son, saying, Give me my wife Mikhal, for whom I paid the bridal price of a hundred foreskins of the Pelishtim.

15 And Ish-Boshet sent and took her from her husband Paltiel the son of Layish.

16 But her husband went with her, weeping after her all the way to Bakhurim. Then Avner said to him, Go, return. And he returned.

17 And Avner conferred with the elders of Yisra'EL, saying, For some time past you have been seeking Dawid as king over you.

18 Now then bring it about, for YHWH has promised Dawid, saying, By the hand of my servant Dawid I will save my people Yisra'EL from the hand of the Pelishtim, and from the hand of all their enemies.

19 Avner also spoke to Binyamin. And then Avner went to tell Dawid at Khevron all that Yisra'EL and the whole house of Binyamin thought good to do.

20 When Avner came with twenty men to Dawid at Khevron, Dawid made a feast for Avner and the men who were with him.

21 And Avner said to Dawid, I will arise and go and will gather all Yisra'EL to my lord the king, that they may make a covenant with you, and that you may reign over all that your heart desires. So Dawid sent Avner away, and he went in peace.

22 Just then the servants of Dawid arrived with Yoav from a raid, bringing much spoil with them. But Avner was not with Dawid at Khevron, for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.

23 When Yoav and all the army that was with him came, it was told Yoav, Avner the son of Ner came to the king, and he sent him away, and he has gone in peace.

24 Then Yoav went to the king and said, What have you done? Behold, Avner came to you. Why is it that you have sent him away, so that he is gone?

25 You know that Avner the son of Ner came to deceive you and to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you are doing.

26 When Yoav came out from Dawid's presence, he sent messengers after Avner, and they brought him back from the cistern of Sirah. But Dawid did not know about it.

27 And when Avner returned to Khevron, Yoav took him aside into the midst of the gate to speak with him privately, and there he struck him in the stomach, so that he died, for the blood of Asah'el his brother.

28 Afterward, when Dawid heard of it, he said, I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before YHWH for the blood of Avner the son of Ner.

29 May it fall upon the head of Yoav and upon all his father's house, and may the house of Yoav never be without one who has a discharge or who is leprous or who holds a spindle or who falls by the sword or who lacks bread.

30 So Yoav and Avishai his brother killed Avner, because he had put their brother Asah'el to death in the battle at Giv'on.

31 Then Dawid said to Yoav and to all the people who were with him, Tear your clothes and put on sackcloth and mourn before Avner. And King Dawid followed the bier.

32 They buried Avner at Khevron. And the king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Avner, and all the people wept.

33 And the king lamented for Avner, saying, Should Avner die as a fool dies?

34 Your hands were not bound, your feet were not fettered — as one falls before the wicked you have fallen. And all the people wept again over him.

35 Then all the people came to persuade Dawid to eat bread while it was yet day. But Dawid swore, saying, Creator do so to me and more also, if I taste bread or anything else till the sun goes down.

36 And all the people took notice of it, and it pleased them, as everything that the king did pleased all the people.

37 So all the people and all Yisra'EL understood that day that it had not been the king's will to put to death Avner the son of Ner.

38 And the king said to his servants, Do you not know that a prince and a great man has fallen this day in Yisra'EL?

39 And I was gentle today, though anointed king. These men, the sons of Tzeruya, are more severe than I. May YHWH repay the evildoer according to his wickedness.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 3. Avner's defection is the covenant turning point of the civil war — the military commander who sustained Ish-Boshet's kingship now declares YHWH's sworn promise to Dawid. His death at Yoav's hand creates the first major crisis of Dawid's kingship: a righteous man killed under his roof without his knowledge.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Dawid's lament for Avner — verse 33-34 — should Avner die as a fool dies? Your hands were not bound — is read as the covenant model of honoring even those who were once enemies. Dawid mourns Avner as a great man regardless of the political history between them.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 3 largely consistent with the Masoretic. Verse 39 — I was gentle today, though anointed king — these men the sons of Tzeruya are more severe than I — is Dawid's honest self-assessment: he knows Yoav has acted beyond covenant bounds and he is not yet strong enough to restrain him.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28-29 — I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before YHWH for the blood of Avner — may it fall upon the head of Yoav. Dawid's formal covenant declaration of innocence is not merely political distancing. He invokes YHWH as the witness of his guiltlessness and calls down covenant consequence upon Yoav. Yet he does not execute Yoav. Verse 39 explains: the sons of Tzeruya are too severe for him to handle in this moment. The covenant king acknowledges his own weakness without shame. The deferred judgment of Yoav will come in Dawid's final instructions to Shelomoh in Melakhim Aleph 2.

THE KINGDOM UNITED — CHAPTERS 4 THROUGH 6

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 4 Ish-Boshet Assassinated — Dawid Executes the Killers — He Who Kills YHWH's Anointed

1 When Ish-Boshet, Sha'ul's son, heard that Avner had died at Khevron, his courage failed, and all Yisra'EL was dismayed.

2 Now Sha'ul's son had two men who were captains of raiding bands — the name of the one was Ba'anah, and the name of the other Rekhav, sons of Rimmon a man of Beeroth, who belonged to Binyamin — for Beeroth also is counted part of Binyamin.

3 The Beerothites fled to Gittayim and have been sojourners there to this day.

4 Yehonatan, Sha'ul's son, had a son who was crippled in his feet. He was five years old when the news about Sha'ul and Yonatan came from Yizre'el, and his nurse took him up and fled, and as she fled in her haste, he fell and became lame. And his name was Mefiboshet.

5 Now the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rekhav and Ba'anah, set out, and about the heat of the day they came to the house of Ish-Boshet as he was taking his noonday rest.

6 And they came into the midst of the house as if to get wheat, and they stabbed him in the stomach. Then Rekhav and Ba'anah his brother escaped.

7 When they came into the house, as he lay on his bed in his bedroom, they struck him and put him to death and beheaded him. They took his head and went by the way of the Aravah all night,

8 And brought the head of Ish-Boshet to Dawid at Khevron. And they said to the king, Here is the head of Ish-Boshet, the son of Sha'ul, your enemy, who sought your life. YHWH has avenged my lord the king this day on Sha'ul and on his offspring.

9 But Dawid answered Rekhav and Ba'anah his brother, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, As YHWH lives, who has redeemed my life out of every adversity,

10 When one told me, Behold, Sha'ul is dead, and thought he was bringing good news, I seized him and killed him at Tziklag — that was the reward I gave him for his news.

11 How much more, when wicked men have killed a righteous man in his own house on his bed, shall I not now require his blood at your hand and destroy you from the earth?

12 And Dawid commanded his young men, and they killed them and cut off their hands and feet and hanged them beside the pool at Khevron. But they took the head of Ish-Boshet and buried it in the tomb of Avner at Khevron.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 4. The brief note about Mefiboshet in verse 4 — Yonatan's son, crippled at five years old when the news of Sha'ul's death arrived — is the covenant seed that will flower in Chapter 9 when Dawid honors his covenant with Yonatan by seeking out his descendants.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Dawid's execution of Ish-Boshet's assassins mirrors his execution of the Amalekite in Chapter 1: both brought him the head of Sha'ul's house expecting reward. Both were executed. The covenant principle is consistent — Dawid will not profit from the killing of YHWH's anointed or his family.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 4 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The covenant pattern of the civil war period: every act that removes an obstacle to Dawid's kingdom — Sha'ul's death, Avner's death, Ish-Boshet's death — comes through acts Dawid did not initiate and expressly mourns or punishes.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9-11 — Dawid's answer is the covenant declaration of his entire approach to the transfer of power. He did not seek Sha'ul's death, Avner's death, or Ish-Boshet's death. When others claim credit for removing his opponents, he executes them rather than rewarding them. The covenant principle: the kingdom that comes through blood-guilt is built on a covenant curse. Dawid receives the kingdom only as YHWH gives it through covenant process, not through assassination politics. Verse 4 — Mefiboshet. The brief introduction of the crippled son of Yonatan in the midst of the political violence is the covenant promise waiting to be remembered.

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 5 All Yisra'EL Anoints Dawid King — Yerushalayim Captured — Dawid Defeats the Pelishtim Twice

1 Then all the tribes of Yisra'EL came to Dawid at Khevron and said, Behold, we are your bone and flesh.

2 In times past, when Sha'ul was king over us, it was you who led out and brought in Yisra'EL. And YHWH said to you, You shall be shepherd of my people Yisra'EL, and you shall be prince over Yisra'EL.

3 So all the elders of Yisra'EL came to the king at Khevron, and King Dawid made a covenant with them at Khevron before YHWH, and they anointed Dawid king over Yisra'EL.

4 Dawid was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.

5 At Khevron he reigned over Yehudah seven years and six months, and at Yerushalayim he reigned over all Yisra'EL and Yehudah thirty-three years.

6 And the king and his men went to Yerushalayim against the Yevusites, the inhabitants of the land, who said to Dawid, You will not come in here, but the blind and the lame will ward you off — thinking, Dawid cannot come in here.

7 Nevertheless, Dawid took the stronghold of Tziyon, that is, the city of Dawid.

8 And Dawid said on that day, Whoever would strike the Yevusites, let him get up the water shaft to attack the lame and the blind, who are hated by Dawid's soul. Therefore it is said, The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.

9 And Dawid lived in the stronghold and called it the city of Dawid. And Dawid built the city all around from the Millo inward.

10 And Dawid became greater and greater, for YHWH, the Creator of hosts, was with him.

11 And Khiram king of Tzor sent messengers to Dawid, and cedar trees, also carpenters and masons who built Dawid a house.

12 And Dawid knew that YHWH had established him king over Yisra'EL, and that he had exalted his kingdom for the sake of his people Yisra'EL.

13 And Dawid took more concubines and wives from Yerushalayim, after he came from Khevron, and more sons and daughters were born to Dawid.

14 And these are the names of those who were born to him in Yerushalayim: Shammua, Shovav, Natan, Shelomoh,

15 Yivkhar, Elishua, Nefeg, Yafiya,

16 Elishama, Elyada, and Eliefelet.

17 When the Pelishtim heard that they had anointed Dawid king over Yisra'EL, all the Pelishtim went up to search for Dawid. But Dawid heard of it and went down to the stronghold.

18 Now the Pelishtim had come and spread out in the Valley of Refa'im.

19 And Dawid inquired of YHWH, Shall I go up against the Pelishtim? Will you give them into my hand? And YHWH said to Dawid, Go up, for I will certainly give the Pelishtim into your hand.

20 And Dawid came to Ba'al-Peratzim, and Dawid defeated them there. And he said, YHWH has broken through my enemies before me like a breaking flood. Therefore the name of that place is called Ba'al-Peratzim.

21 And the Pelishtim left their idols there, and Dawid and his men carried them away.

22 And the Pelishtim came up yet again and spread out in the Valley of Refa'im.

23 And when Dawid inquired of YHWH, he said, You shall not go up — go around to their rear and come against them opposite the balsam trees.

24 And when you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then rouse yourself, for then YHWH has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Pelishtim.

25 And Dawid did as YHWH commanded him, and struck down the Pelishtim from Geva to Gezer.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 5. The anointing of Dawid as king over all Yisra'EL — after seven and a half years as king of Yehudah alone — is the covenant fulfillment of the anointing by Shemu'el in 1 Shemu'el 16. The covenant process from private anointing to public coronation spans twenty years.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 2 — YHWH said to you, you shall be shepherd of my people Yisra'EL — the covenant title for Dawid is shepherd, not warrior or king. The pastoral image defines the covenant character of Dawid's kingship as service rather than domination.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 5 largely consistent with the Masoretic. The two battles against the Pelishtim — verses 17-25 — both begin with Dawid inquiring of YHWH. The second battle receives a different command than the first: do not go up the same way — go around and wait for the sound in the balsam trees. Dawid does not assume the same approach will work twice.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 and 23 — both times Dawid inquires of YHWH before engaging the Pelishtim. This is the covenant discipline that marks Dawid's military leadership: every major decision goes to YHWH before implementation. The second battle commands a different strategy — go around, attack from the rear, wait for the signal. YHWH's instructions are specific, not generic. Verse 20 — YHWH has broken through my enemies before me like a breaking flood. Dawid names the battle after YHWH's action — Ba'al-Peratzim — the master of breaking through. The king attributes the victory to YHWH by naming the place after it.

SHEMU'EL BET — CHAPTER 6 The Ark Brought to Yerushalayim — Uzzah Struck Dead — Dawid Dances Before YHWH — Mikhal's Contempt

1 Dawid again gathered all the chosen men of Yisra'EL, thirty thousand.

2 And Dawid arose and went with all the people who were with him from Ba'alei-Yehudah to bring up from there the Ark of Creator, which is called by the name of YHWH of hosts who sits enthroned on the cherubim.

3 And they carried the Ark of Creator on a new cart and brought it out of the house of Avina'dav, which was on the hill. And Uzzah and Akhyo, the sons of Avina'dav, were driving the new cart,

4 With the Ark of Creator, and Akhyo went before the Ark.

5 And Dawid and all the house of Yisra'EL were celebrating before YHWH, with songs and lyres and harps and tambourines and castanets and cymbals.

6 And when they came to the threshing floor of Nakhon, Uzzah put out his hand to the Ark of Creator and took hold of it, for the oxen stumbled.

7 And the anger of YHWH was kindled against Uzzah, and Creator struck him down there because of his error, and he died there beside the Ark of Creator.

8 And Dawid was angry because YHWH had broken out against Uzzah. And that place is called Perez-Uzzah, to this day.

9 And Dawid was afraid of YHWH that day, and he said, How can the Ark of YHWH come to me?

10 So Dawid was not willing to take the Ark of YHWH into the city of Dawid. But Dawid took it aside to the house of Oved-Edom the Gittite.

11 And the Ark of YHWH remained in the house of Oved-Edom the Gittite three months, and YHWH blessed Oved-Edom and all his household.

12 And it was told King Dawid, YHWH has blessed the household of Oved-Edom and all that belongs to him, because of the Ark of Creator. So Dawid went and brought up the Ark of Creator from the house of Oved-Edom to the city of Dawid with rejoicing.

13 And when those who bore the Ark of YHWH had gone six steps, he sacrificed an ox and a fattened animal.

14 And Dawid danced before YHWH with all his might. And Dawid was wearing a linen ephod.

15 So Dawid and all the house of Yisra'EL brought up the Ark of YHWH with shouting and with the sound of the horn.

16 As the Ark of YHWH came into the city of Dawid, Mikhal the daughter of Sha'ul looked out of the window and saw King Dawid leaping and dancing before YHWH, and she despised him in her heart.

17 And they brought in the Ark of YHWH and set it in its place, inside the tent that Dawid had pitched for it. And Dawid offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before YHWH.

18 And when Dawid had finished offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of YHWH of hosts

19 And distributed among all the people, the whole multitude of Yisra'EL, both men and women, a cake of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins to each one. Then all the people departed, each to his house.

20 And Dawid returned to bless his household. But Mikhal the daughter of Sha'ul came out to meet Dawid and said, How the king of Yisra'EL honored himself today, uncovering himself today before the eyes of his servants' female servants, as one of the vulgar fellows shamelessly uncovers himself.

21 And Dawid said to Mikhal, It was before YHWH, who chose me above your father and above all his house, to appoint me as prince over Yisra'EL, the people of YHWH — and I will celebrate before YHWH.

22 I will make myself yet more contemptible than this, and I will be abased in your eyes. But by the female servants of whom you have spoken, by them I shall be held in honor.

23 And Mikhal the daughter of Sha'ul had no child to the day of her death.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QSama preserves portions of Chapter 6. The death of Uzzah — verse 7 — is the covenant demonstration that YHWH's holiness cannot be handled carelessly even with good intentions. The Ark was to be carried on poles by Levites — Bamidbar 4:15 — not transported on a cart. Uzzah's death is the consequence of a procedural covenant violation, not moral evil.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Dawid's dancing before YHWH — verse 14 — with all his might, wearing only a linen ephod — is read as the covenant expression of complete self-abandonment before YHWH. The king removes the dignity of his office to worship with the abandon of a servant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QSama preserves Chapter 6 largely consistent with the Masoretic. Mikhal's contempt and her subsequent childlessness — verse 20-23 — are the covenant consequence of despising covenant worship. She inherited her father Sha'ul's tendency to evaluate YHWH's ways by human standards of dignity and decorum.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21-22 — it was before YHWH who chose me above your father — I will make myself yet more contemptible than this. Dawid's response to Mikhal's contempt is one of the most direct covenant declarations in Shemu'el Bet. He is not dancing for Mikhal's approval or the court's admiration. He dances before YHWH. The covenant worship is directed at YHWH alone, and Dawid is willing to be further humiliated in Mikhal's eyes if that is the cost of covenant abandon before YHWH. Verse 23 — Mikhal had no child to the day of her death. The covenant consequence is stated without commentary. The woman who despised Dawid's worship before YHWH received no covenant fruit.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 6
